revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=琴平神社| 所在地=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|}}| 祭神=大物主...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=琴平神社| 所在地=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|}}| 祭神=大物主命| 創建=江戸時代前期| 社格=| 例祭=| 本殿=一間社入母屋造| 画像=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿}} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。祭神は[[大物主|大物主命]]。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金比羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋]]造り平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 ==文化財== {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市]]

{{神社| 名称=琴平神社| 所在地=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|}}| 祭神=大物主命| 創建=江戸時代前期| 社格=| 例祭=| 本殿=一間社入母屋造| 画像=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿}} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。祭神は[[大物主|大物主命]]。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金比羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 ==文化財== {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市]]

{{神社| 名称=琴平神社| 所在地=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=琴平神社}}| 祭神=大物主命| 創建=江戸時代前期| 社格=| 例祭=| 本殿=一間社入母屋造| 画像=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿}} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。祭神は[[大物主|大物主命]]。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金比羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 ==文化財== {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:都道府県指定有形文化財]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年、地元の人々により石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、この前面に金毘羅大権現と彫られている。なお、石の鳥居の扁額は琴平神社であるので祭神が[[大物主|大物主命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、祭神が[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、祭神が[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、祭神が[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 概要 ==" to "== Overview =="
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | |name=琴平神社 (土佐市)|native_name=琴平神社 | address=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566 | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=琴平神社}} | deity=大物主命 | 創建=江戸時代前期 | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=一間社入母屋造 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == Overview == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == Overview == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == Cultural Properties == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006]] 224ページ == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[sn:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}}

{{infobox religious building | |name=琴平神社 (土佐市)|native_name=琴平神社 | address=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566 | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=琴平神社}} | deity=大物主命 | 創建=江戸時代前期 | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=一間社入母屋造 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]

本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == Cultural Properties == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == Overview == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == Cultural Properties == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006]] 224ページ == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:untranslated shrines and temples]] [[sn:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}}

{{infobox religious building | |name=琴平神社 (土佐市)|native_name=琴平神社 | address=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566 | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=琴平神社}} | deity=大物主命 | 創建=江戸時代前期 | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=一間社入母屋造 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == Overview == [[Bunka]]12年（[[1815]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、deityが[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == Cultural Properties == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006]] 224ページ == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:untranslated shrines and temples]] [[sn:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)}}

{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Kohtohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]]

Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市|12=draft}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所|12=draft}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国|12=draft}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|12=draft}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|12=draft}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=draft}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|12=draft}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|12=draft}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命|12=draft}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|12=draft}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|12=draft}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|12=draft}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=琴平神社 | 所在地=高知県土佐市高岡町丁566 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=琴平神社}} | 祭神=大物主命 | 創建=江戸時代前期 | 社格= | 例祭= | 本殿=一間社入母屋造 | 画像=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
本殿 }} '''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[土佐市]]にある[[神社]]。[[四国八十八箇所]]三十五番札所[[清瀧寺 (土佐市)|清瀧寺]]の境内にある。 == 概要 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（[[1815年]]）に武藤到和・平道の父子により編纂された[[土佐国]]地誌『南路志』によれば、[[江戸時代]]前期の[[寛文]]8年（[[1668年]]）に清瀧寺本堂が全焼した。寺院の防火および発展を祈願して金毘羅が勧進されたと書かれている。これが当神社の起源と考えられており、創建は江戸時代前期17世紀末期と推定される。 本殿は一間社[[入母屋造]]平入り柿葺きで、正面に一間の向拝が付いている。「奉再建山神宮」の棟札に[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）の銘がある。江戸時代後期のこの年に建造されたと推定されている。現在、被屋で保護されている。[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）に高知県の保護有形文化財に指定された。 平成10年3月、地元の人々により石の鳥居と石灯篭一対が新しく設置され、鳥居の扁額は琴平神社で灯篭には金毘羅大権現と彫られているので、祭神が[[大物主|大物主と命]]であるか[[金毘羅権現]]であるかは定かでない。 == 文化財 == *本殿：高知県保護有形文化財　平成8年（1996年）[[4月30日]]指定 File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|全景 File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|向かって左の彫刻 File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|向かって右の彫刻 == 参考文献 == *高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会/編 『高知県の歴史散歩』 [[山川出版社]]/刊 [[2006年]] 224ページ == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 高知県の観光情報ガイド「よさこいネット」（琴平神社）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:土佐市の建築物]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:高知県指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}}

''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|12=draft}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{citation needed|kotohirashinja}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{citation needed\|[^]]+}}" to ""
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市|qq=draft}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺|qq=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国|qq=draft}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:11-January 4 (Old Calendar)-11]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tosa City|ja|土佐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa City|qq=}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|ja|四国八十八箇所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|qq=}}, {{ill|Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|ja|清瀧寺 (土佐市)|lt=Seiryu-ji|lt_ja=清瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryu-ji (Tosa City)|qq=}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Province|qq=}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Bunka|ja|文化 (元号)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1815|qq=}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1668|qq=}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1831|qq=}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998|qq=}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Oomononushi|ja|大物主|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=大物主と命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomononushi|qq=}} or {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 30|qq=}} in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]]

{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]]. == Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1815|qq=}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1668|qq=}}) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1831|qq=}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998|qq=}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]]. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]]. == Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1815|qq=}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1668|qq=}}) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1831|qq=}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998|qq=}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]]. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa, Kochi (city)]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kochi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]]. == Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1815|qq=}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1668|qq=}}) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1831|qq=}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998|qq=}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]]. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa City]] [[Category:History of Tosa City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa, Kochi (city)]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kochi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}}

Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]]. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages Needing Dummy Edit for Attribution and to be Reviewed if a Redirect and Jawiki Link Reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa, Kochi (city)]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kochi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=[[File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG|260px]]
Main Hall }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]]. == Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ({{ill|1815|ja|1815|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1815|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ({{ill|1668|ja|1668|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1668|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ({{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1831|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture. In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ({{ill|1998|ja|1998|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1998|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]]. == Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}). File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right == References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=}} in {{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}, 224 pages == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{translated page|ja|琴平神社 (土佐市)|version=95143005|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Kochi City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Tosa, Kochi (city)]] [[Category:土佐市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kochi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}}

== Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ([[:en:1815|1815]]) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ([[:en:1668|1668]]) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ([[:en:1998|1998]]), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]].
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573775]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG | prefecture={{ill|Tosa|WD=Q851163}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.512639|N|133.409778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ([[:en:1815|1815]]) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ([[:en:1668|1668]]) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ([[:en:1998|1998]]), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]].
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]).
