[[Category:Buildings in Tosa, Kochi (city)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kochi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} {{wikidata link|Q11573775}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573775]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG | prefecture={{ill|Tosa|WD=Q851163}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.512639|N|133.409778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ([[:en:1815|1815]]) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ([[:en:1668|1668]]) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ([[:en:1998|1998]]), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]].
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ([[:en:1815|1815]]) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ([[:en:1668|1668]]) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ([[:en:1998|1998]]), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]].
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]).
<gallery> File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right </gallery>
== References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}} in [[:en:2006|2006]], 224 pages
== External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573775]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image=File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG | prefecture={{ill|Tosa|WD=Q851163}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.512639|N|133.409778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]] [[:en:Tosa, Kōchi|Tosa City]]. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the [[:en:Shikoku Pilgrimage|Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites]], [[:en:Kiyotaki-ji|Seiryu-ji]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]] local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in [[:en:Culture|Bunka]] 12 ([[:en:1815|1815]]) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 8 ([[:en:1668|1668]]) during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 10 ([[:en:1998|1998]]), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]], it remains uncertain whether the deity is [[:en:Ōmononushi|Oomononushi-no-mikoto]] or [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Daigongen]].
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 8 ([[:en:1996|1996]]).
<gallery> File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right </gallery>
== References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}} in [[:en:2006|2006]], 224 pages
== External Links == {{commons|Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)}} *[http://www.attaka.or.jp/kanko/dtl.php?ID=218 Kochi Prefecture Tourism Information Guide "Yosakoi Net" (Kotohira Shrine)] {{Shinto navigation}}

== Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 8 ({{ill|1668|en|1668|WD=Q7595|lt=1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.

Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto}} or {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen}}.

== Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 8 ({{ill|1668|en|1668|WD=Q7595|lt=1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 10 ({{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto}} or {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen}}.
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30}} in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}).
<gallery> File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right </gallery>

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Tosa, Kōchi|en|Tosa, Kōchi|WD=Q851163|lt=Tosa City|1=Tosa, Kōchi}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}, {{ill|Kiyotaki-ji|en|Kiyotaki-ji|WD=Q11561323|lt=Seiryu-ji|1=Kiyotaki-ji}}.
== Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 12 ({{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815|1=1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 8 ({{ill|1668|en|1668|WD=Q7595|lt=1668|1=1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 10 ({{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} or {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}.
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}).
<gallery> File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right </gallery>

''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}} in {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}}, 224 pages

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kotohira-jinja (Tosa)]][[ja:琴平神社 (土佐市)]]
{{infobox religious building | name=Kotohira Shrine (Tosa City) | native_name=琴平神社 | address=566 Takaoka-cho, Tosa City, Kochi Prefecture | location={{coordinates|33|30|45.5|N|133|24|35.2|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine}} | deity=Ōmononushi | Founded=Early Edo period | 社格= | festival= | 本殿=Single-bay shrine with Irimoya-zukuri | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image=File:Tosa Kotohira Jinja 03.JPG | prefecture={{ill|Tosa|WD=Q851163|1=Tosa, Kōchi}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.512639|N|133.409778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Tosa, Kōchi|en|Tosa, Kōchi|WD=Q851163|lt=Tosa City|1=Tosa, Kōchi}}. It is situated within the precincts of the 35th site of the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku 88 Pilgrimage Sites|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}, {{ill|Kiyotaki-ji|en|Kiyotaki-ji|WD=Q11561323|lt=Seiryu-ji|1=Kiyotaki-ji}}.
== Overview == According to the {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}} local history "Nanro-shi", compiled in {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|WD=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}} 12 ({{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815|1=1815}}) by the father and son Mutō Tōwa and Heidō, the main hall of Seiryu-ji burned down entirely in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 8 ({{ill|1668|en|1668|WD=Q7595|lt=1668|1=1668}}) during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. It is recorded that {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} was enshrined to pray for fire prevention and the temple's development. This event is considered the origin of the shrine, and its founding is estimated to have occurred in the late 17th century of the early Edo period.
The main hall is a single-bay shrine constructed in {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style with a cypress bark roof and features a one-bay approach hall at the front. The ridge plaque of the "Hōsaikenshinyama Shrine" bears an inscription dating to {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}), indicating that it was built in that year during the late Edo period. It is currently safeguarded by a protective covering structure. In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}), the building was designated as a tangible cultural property protected by Kochi Prefecture.
In March of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 10 ({{ill|1998|en|1998|WD=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} or {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}.
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}).
<gallery> File:Kotohirajinja20211126 1.jpg|Overall view File:Kotohira 20220913 2.jpg|Sculpture on the left File:Kotohira 20220913 3.jpg|Sculpture on the right </gallery>
== References == * Kochi Prefectural High School Education Research Association History Committee, ed. ''A Historical Walk in Kochi Prefecture'', published by {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}} in {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}}, 224 pages

In March of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 10 ({{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}}), local residents installed a new stone torii and a pair of stone lanterns. Since the torii’s inscription reads "Kotohira Shrine" and the lanterns are carved with {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|qid=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}, it remains uncertain whether the deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|lt=Oomononushi-no-mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} or {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|qid=Q167077|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=Konpira Gongen}}.
== Cultural Properties == *Main Hall: Designated tangible cultural property of Kochi Prefecture, designated on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|qid=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}).
